meaning: Only abstract/title/metadata were checked; use only for triage.
when_to_use: Use when the PDF was not opened but the paper remains in the sprint.
promotion_effect: Counts as a started note, but is weak evidence for claim promotion.

meaning: Main paper was skimmed but not fully checked.
when_to_use: Use after checking intro/method/results enough to summarize the contribution.
promotion_effect: Counts as a note; reviewer should mark evidence_strength conservatively.

meaning: Main paper body was read; appendix may remain unchecked.
when_to_use: Default target for sprint review.
promotion_effect: Good enough for most claim/area overlay decisions.

meaning: Main paper and relevant appendix/artifact details were checked.
when_to_use: Use for artifact, theory, benchmark, or controversial claim examples.

meaning: Paper could not be reviewed because source access or content was unavailable.
when_to_use: Use only with a reason in paper_note.

meaning: Paper primarily opens or reframes a problem.
when_to_use: Use when contribution is mostly problem definition, benchmark framing, or position.
promotion_effect: Can support trend/fault-line claims but not necessarily method-strength claims.

meaning: Paper introduces a new mechanism, algorithm, architecture, or training recipe.
when_to_use: Use when the technical mechanism is the main contribution.

meaning: Paper primarily contributes theorem/proof/analysis strength.
when_to_use: Use for theory or formal-analysis papers.
promotion_effect: Useful for program-attention and theory-area claims.

meaning: Paper primarily improves efficiency, scale, tooling, or systems integration.
when_to_use: Use for infrastructure, serving, tooling, or agent workflow papers.

meaning: Paper primarily contributes benchmark/data/evaluation packaging.
when_to_use: Use when benchmark/data are central rather than a side artifact.
promotion_effect: Can support public-attention or reproducibility claims with caveats.

meaning: Contribution appears mostly incremental relative to cited baselines.
when_to_use: Use when evidence does not justify headline-example status.

meaning: Reviewer cannot confidently classify novelty.
when_to_use: Use when the paper needs a second read or specialist review.
promotion_effect: Blocks promotion unless other reviewed rows are strong.

meaning: Claim is backed by credible baselines, metrics, ablations, and limitations.
when_to_use: Use when evidence would survive direct citation in a report.
promotion_effect: Good candidate for support_rows when claim implication supports the claim.

meaning: Evidence is useful but has gaps or limited scope.
when_to_use: Default for credible but not definitive papers.

meaning: Evidence is thin, undercompared, or mostly qualitative.
when_to_use: Use when paper should be a caveat or low-confidence example.
promotion_effect: Usually should not count as strong support.

meaning: Evidence complicates, narrows, or contradicts a simple claim.
when_to_use: Use when results show limits, failure modes, or mixed outcomes.
promotion_effect: May become a weakening row or caveat example.

meaning: Evidence could not be classified.
when_to_use: Use when source did not provide enough information in checked sections.

meaning: No artifact link or release was found.
when_to_use: Use after checking metadata/PDF sufficiently.
promotion_effect: Weakens artifact-visibility claims when a release is expected.

meaning: Artifact link exists but was not opened or inspected.
when_to_use: Use only as a triage state.
promotion_effect: Does not satisfy artifact check gates.

meaning: Artifact/repository link was opened and exists.
when_to_use: Use after checking live status, license/release details optional.
promotion_effect: Counts toward artifact inspection but not runnable status.

meaning: Artifact appears runnable or includes clear reproduction instructions.
when_to_use: Use only after inspecting repository contents.

meaning: Artifact link is stale, private, missing, or unusable.
when_to_use: Use after a direct check.

meaning: Artifact is not expected for this paper type.
when_to_use: Use for pure theory/position papers or cases where release is irrelevant.

meaning: Current area/subarea assignment is acceptable.
when_to_use: Use when taxonomy evidence matches the paper's main contribution.

meaning: Paper belongs in a different report-level area.
when_to_use: Use for clear area mismatch.
promotion_effect: Blocks taxonomy-sensitive claim promotion until resolved.

meaning: Area is acceptable but subarea should change.
when_to_use: Use for boundary or submode corrections.

meaning: Paper genuinely spans multiple areas/subareas.
when_to_use: Use when no single assignment is clearly dominant.
promotion_effect: Use as a caveat for aggregate area claims.

meaning: Taxonomy cannot be decided from reviewed material.
when_to_use: Use when reviewer needs more context.

meaning: Paper can anchor a report claim.
when_to_use: Use only when contribution and evidence are clear.

meaning: Paper supports a claim but should not anchor it.
when_to_use: Use for useful but less central examples.

meaning: Paper mainly complicates or narrows a claim.
when_to_use: Use when it exposes limits or counterexamples.
promotion_effect: Useful for critical analysis; may weaken promotion.

meaning: Paper should not be used in final report synthesis.
when_to_use: Use for irrelevant, weak, or misclassified examples.

meaning: Reviewer has not decided report role.
when_to_use: Use when additional review is needed.

meaning: Paper materially supports the linked synthesis claim.
when_to_use: Use when reviewed evidence directly supports the claim wording.
promotion_effect: Counts as support in acceptance criteria.

meaning: Paper supports a narrower or caveated version of the claim.
when_to_use: Use when the claim is directionally right but too broad.
promotion_effect: Counts as support, but manual_notes should carry caveat.

meaning: Paper complicates or weakens the linked claim.
when_to_use: Use for contrary evidence, scope limits, or mismatch.
promotion_effect: Counts as weakening; blocks promotion while unresolved.

meaning: Paper directly contradicts the linked claim.
when_to_use: Use for clear counterexamples.
promotion_effect: Counts as weakening; should trigger claim revision.

meaning: Paper should not be used for this claim.
when_to_use: Use when paper is in queue but does not bear on the claim.

meaning: Reviewer cannot decide claim relationship.
when_to_use: Use when further review is needed.

meaning: Taxonomy assignment is acceptable for this claim.
when_to_use: Use when area/subarea supports the claim linkage.

meaning: Claim/taxonomy grouping is overly broad for this paper.
when_to_use: Use when the paper fits only a narrower slice.

meaning: Claim/taxonomy grouping misses broader relevance.
when_to_use: Use when paper spans more than assigned category.

meaning: Report-level area assignment is wrong.
when_to_use: Use for clear area mismatch.

meaning: Subarea assignment is wrong but area may be acceptable.
when_to_use: Use for subarea mismatch.
promotion_effect: Blocks subarea claims and adds caveat to area claims.

meaning: Taxonomy relationship is unresolved.
when_to_use: Use when taxonomy needs another reviewer.

meaning: Artifact check is irrelevant to this claim/paper.
when_to_use: Use for theory/position or non-artifact claims.

meaning: No artifact found.
when_to_use: Use after checking source links.

meaning: Artifact link exists but was not inspected.
when_to_use: Use as a temporary state.

meaning: Artifact link was opened and is live.
when_to_use: Use after a live check.

meaning: Artifact appears runnable/reproducible.
when_to_use: Use after inspecting setup or examples.

meaning: Artifact link or repository is unusable.
when_to_use: Use after direct inspection.
promotion_effect: Counts as checked but weakens artifact claim.

meaning: Paper validates assigned area/subarea and area claim use.
when_to_use: Use when assignment and evidence are clear.

meaning: Paper partly validates area use but needs caveat.
when_to_use: Use for boundary papers.

meaning: Paper should not validate the assigned area/fault line.
when_to_use: Use for misclassified or irrelevant examples.

meaning: Area validation is unresolved.
when_to_use: Use when more review is needed.

meaning: Main contribution is an algorithm, architecture, model, or training method.
when_to_use: Use for mechanism-first papers.

meaning: Main contribution is formal analysis/proof.
when_to_use: Use for theory papers.

meaning: Main contribution is infrastructure, serving, tooling, or integrated system.
when_to_use: Use for systems/agents workflows.

meaning: Main contribution is data collection, dataset, or resource.
when_to_use: Use when data resource is central.

meaning: Main contribution is benchmark/evaluation suite.
when_to_use: Use for evaluation-package papers.

meaning: Main contribution is empirical or conceptual analysis.
when_to_use: Use for diagnostic/measurement papers.
promotion_effect: Often supports caveats or fault lines.

meaning: Main contribution is an applied use case.
when_to_use: Use when method novelty is secondary.
promotion_effect: Supports area breadth, not necessarily core method claims.

meaning: Main contribution is position, survey, or argument.
when_to_use: Use for position papers.
promotion_effect: Useful for context, not empirical support.

meaning: Contribution type cannot be decided.
when_to_use: Use when review is inconclusive.

meaning: No artifact found.
when_to_use: Use after checking.

meaning: Artifact link exists but was not inspected.
when_to_use: Use as temporary state.

meaning: Artifact link is live.
when_to_use: Use after opening the link.

meaning: Artifact appears runnable or reproducible.
when_to_use: Use after inspecting setup/data/checkpoints.

meaning: Artifact is stale, private, or unusable.
when_to_use: Use after failed direct check.

meaning: Artifact is irrelevant to paper type.
when_to_use: Use for pure theory/position papers.

meaning: Results primarily strengthen the area/fault-line story.
when_to_use: Use for clear wins or validated results.

meaning: Results primarily show failures or limits.
when_to_use: Use for negative or cautionary papers.

meaning: Results are mixed across settings.
when_to_use: Use when paper should narrow a claim.

meaning: Result is mainly theoretical.
when_to_use: Use for theory/proof papers.

meaning: Result is mainly benchmark/data packaging.
when_to_use: Use for dataset/benchmark papers.

meaning: Result character cannot be decided.
when_to_use: Use when additional reading is needed.

meaning: Paper is strong enough to anchor an area fault line.
when_to_use: Use sparingly for best examples.

meaning: Paper supports but should not anchor the fault line.
when_to_use: Use for secondary examples.

meaning: Paper mainly adds a caveat or boundary condition.
when_to_use: Use for limitations, failures, or mixed evidence.

meaning: Paper should not be used for this area fault line.
when_to_use: Use for irrelevant/misclassified rows.

meaning: Fault-line relevance is unresolved.
when_to_use: Use when another review pass is needed.
